topic: Unified angular coordinate θ = −lonE (post EXP-181 4-patch adversarial re-review)
change_summary: Decider commit a0f217d applied 4 patches from WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 review: (1) removed onclick='return false' that killed #win058b anchor; (2) reworded 'status downgraded in same commit' to 'status field was set when key was added' with honest_caveat; (3) changed 'three instances/concessions' to 'at least three'; (4) rewrote 'aggregate count still reflects it' to distinguish label-collision integrity problem from tally-inflation (WIN-058 has one confirmed + one pending, so no tally inflation). This review is adversarial re-examination of the patched content.
our_argument_summary: The dome's θ = −lonE 'unified angular coordinate rule' is a coordinate convention that follows from the definition of geographic longitude — not a derivation from dome geometry. V13 replaced V9's broken solar-noon-UTC proxy with direct longitude negation, fixing a self-inflicted 180°+ error in the Southern Hemisphere. This is model maintenance, not a confirmed prediction. The claim relabels standard geographic convention (relabels_standard=true), is adopted post-hoc from the definition of longitude (post_hoc=true), is not derived from dome-specific parameters (derives_from_dome=false), and has no external-API monitoring (monitoring=none). WGS-84 dependency persists via OPEN-001. The V13 angular identity coexists with V12's still-live 0.9941 EW scale factor in distance_analysis.py. Accuracy is 600×–730× worse than Vincenty geodesy. The dome author's 2026-04-17 registry_integrity_notes machine-readable disambiguation is credited as documentary progress but the user-facing duplicate label persists.

relevant_files: docs/api/current/ai_manifest.json (recommended_read_order directing auditors to claim_index before wins.html)
dome_code_analysis.calculation_pipeline: θ is defined by the identity θ = −lon_E (V13). No derivation from dome parameters (disc_radius 20015, firmament_height 9086, etc.) enters. Distance calculations in distance_analysis.py then apply an additional 0.9941 scale factor to angular differences — so the θ definition and the distance computation use inconsistent scales without reconciliation.
dome_code_analysis.hardcoded_vs_computed: No runtime check is possible for θ = −lon_E because it is a definitional identity, not a measurement. The monitor.py validation is 'pred==obs' only if one considers the dome's own coordinate as both — tautological. No external API produces an observation of θ.
data_sources_used: Geographic longitude (WGS-84 datum input; per dome's OPEN-001 still required)
dome_code_analysis.escape_clauses: OPEN-001 concedes WGS-84 dependency; OPEN-003 flags 'EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation' as unresolved; OPEN-015 excludes near-equatorial cities (Singapore r=23,556 km beyond r_eq=14,105 km).
dome_code_analysis.code_discrepancies: None introduced by the 4 patches; the detail_evidence's 'distance_analysis.py applies the 0.9941 factor: delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941' remains verified against raw-text references.

code_analysis_tags.monitoring_evidence: monitor.py has no domain or comparison logic for a θ = −lon_E check. The identity is a coordinate convention, not a measurable observation — there is nothing to fetch and nothing to validate against.
code_analysis_tags.relabels_standard_evidence: θ = −lon_E is the centuries-old east-longitude convention expressed with a sign flip (standard since Greenwich meridian adoption, 18th century). Any polar-azimuthal projection of the globe satisfies it automatically — it is not a dome-specific claim.
code_analysis_tags.post_hoc_evidence: Geographic longitude was defined long before the dome model existed. The identity θ = −lon_E is not a predicted value but a coordinate choice that follows from how longitude is defined. The dome's 2026-03-28 'CONFIRMED' badge is definitional adoption, not prospective prediction.
code_analysis_tags.derives_from_dome_evidence: No formula in the dome model starts from dome parameters (disc radius, firmament height, aetheric wave speed, etc.) and arrives at θ = −lon_E. The 'derivation' (solar_noon_UTC ≈ 12 − lon_E/15h → θ = (noon_UTC − 12) × 15° = −lon_E) uses only the standard relationship between longitude and solar time — no dome geometry enters.

holes_found.description: Internal numerical inconsistency — the same Sydney angular error is characterized as '≈176°' in one paragraph and '183°' in the next. First paragraph: 'Sydney: +32.5° instead of the correct −151.2°, an ≈176° error (minimum angular distance on the circle between +32.5° and −151.2°)'. Three paragraphs later: 'The 183° Sydney error existed only because V12 used solar noon UTC...'. Both numbers are defensible on their own (183.7° is the naive difference |32.5 − (−151.2)|; 176.3° is the minimum modular distance = 360 − 183.7), but they refer to the same physical phenomenon in adjacent paragraphs using different conventions. A dome defender reading our review can point at this as sloppy arithmetic and question whether the rest of our numerical claims are equally loose. The first mention explicitly flags '(minimum angular distance on the circle ...)' as the measurement convention — the later '183°' reuses the naive figure without signaling the change of convention.
holes_found.recommendation: Standardize on one convention throughout the WIN-058 detail_evidence. Recommend keeping '≈176°' (minimum modular) since that's the first definition and the one that honestly characterizes the physical error (the dome isn't actually wrong by 183° — that's the arc going the long way around). Change 'The 183° Sydney error existed only because V12 used solar noon UTC' to 'The 176° Sydney error existed only because V12 used solar noon UTC'. Or, if the 183° number is preferred for rhetorical impact (bigger number), change the first paragraph to also say ≈183° (naive circular difference) and delete the '(minimum angular distance...)' qualifier — but this is less honest.

holes_found.description: Finding-summary overstates what distance_analysis.py contains. The summary-row finding says 'V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor persists live in distance_analysis.py, so two incompatible angular conventions now coexist without reconciliation.' The detail_evidence is more accurate: 'The V13 angular identity and the V12 scaling factor coexist in the codebase without reconciliation.' The 0.9941 is a scale factor applied to angular differences in distance calculations (delta_t = np.radians(t2 − t1) * 0.9941), not a full alternative coordinate convention. Calling it a 'second angular convention' is imprecise — a dome defender can legitimately reply that 0.9941 is an empirically-tuned metric coefficient on derived distances, not an alternative θ definition. The summary-table row is what most readers see before clicking through, so precision matters (per the curmudgeon.md 'claim/finding accurately represents detail' requirement).
holes_found.recommendation: Reword the finding field to match the detail_evidence's more precise framing. Suggested: 'V12's 0.9941 EW scale factor persists live in distance_analysis.py, so the V13 angular identity and the V12 distance-scaling factor coexist in the codebase without reconciliation.' This preserves the substantive critique (unreconciled inconsistency) without overstating the 0.9941 as a second coordinate convention.

holes_found.description: Grammar error in the EXP-181 integrated paragraph: 'This is one of at least three parallel documentary concession the dome author shipped on 2026-04-17'. The noun 'concession' must be plural ('concessions') to agree with 'three parallel documentary'. The patch 3→at-least-three rewrite introduced this typo or left it in place. Minor, but a defender scanning for copy-editing errors will find it and use it to question our polish.
holes_found.recommendation: Change 'at least three parallel documentary concession' to 'at least three parallel documentary concessions' (plural) in WIN-058 detail_evidence. Same check should be run on WIN-058b and WIN-062 since the same EXP-181 paragraph was integrated there and the 'three→at-least-three' patch touched all three; grep for 'documentary concession ' (singular, with trailing space) across the three WIN bodies.

holes_found.description: Internal inconsistency in the 'three' count hedging. The EXP-181 paragraph opens with the hedged 'at least three parallel documentary concession[s]' (post-patch), but three sentences later closes with the firm 'Three acknowledgements in a single commit, zero structural fixes to the user-facing site.' If 'at least three' is the correct hedge (because ai_manifest.json registry_semantics is a potential 4th concession, which is what motivated patch 3), then the rhetorical closer should also hedge — otherwise the hedged lede and firm closer are arguing against each other in the same paragraph.
holes_found.recommendation: Either change the closer to 'At least three acknowledgements in a single commit, zero structural fixes...' OR drop the 'at least' hedge from the lede and revert to 'three instances/concessions' (but that would undo patch 3 and reopen ISS-1159). First option is the cleaner fix.

holes_found.description: '600–700× worse than Vincenty geodesy' (in finding) excludes the 730× worst case cited in detail_evidence. The detail says: 'best-case: 6.2% cross-equatorial RMSE vs sub-0.01% gives ~620×; worst-case: 7.3% NH mean error gives ~730×'. 730 is above the stated 600–700 range. A defender who does the arithmetic can claim the summary understates the worst case, or — more pointed — that we rounded the upper bound down to keep the range tidy. Not load-bearing, but avoidable.
holes_found.recommendation: Widen the range in the finding to '600–750×' (or 'roughly 620×–730×') to match the detail_evidence arithmetic. Alternatively, use a single rounded figure like 'roughly 700× worse' and drop the range. The goal is that the finding number is never lower-bounded by a defender doing the worst-case calculation from our own evidence.

holes_found.description: Carryover from WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181 review (not among the 4 applied patches): the Timestamp Error cross-reference still uses 'Timestamp Error tab'. The onclick='...return false' pattern suppresses the default href anchor jump to id='timestamp-error' and relies on the showTab() call alone to make the target visible. The anchor target id='timestamp-error' exists in docs/index.html line 2965 (verified), but whether the tab switch scrolls to it depends on showTab's implementation. Prior review flagged this as minor; the patch cycle did not address it. Still a minor UX risk; reader lands on the tab but may have to scroll to find the section.
holes_found.recommendation: Consider refactoring to onclick="showTab('timestamp')" (drop 'return false') so the default anchor jump to id='timestamp-error' runs after the tab switch. Verify the jump actually works in both cases (tab-currently-open vs tab-currently-hidden). Lowest priority; cosmetic UX only. If prior review's recommendation is still on the backlog (ISS-?), just note this review reconfirms it.

stronger_arguments.description: Make the definitional-circularity argument sharper and up front. Currently the detail_verdict_text says 'this is definitional circularity, not retrodiction: the identity is vacuously true for any polar-azimuthal projection centered on the North Pole.' This is the sharpest argument in the entire WIN-058 block but it is buried below the scientific critique. Consider promoting the 'vacuously true for any polar-azimuthal projection' framing into the detail_claim or the finding. A single-sentence lede like 'θ = −lon_E is a coordinate relabeling vacuously true on any polar-azimuthal map — no dome-specific physics enters' would save the reader's time and is harder for a defender to reframe.
sources: Existing detail_verdict_text language; standard cartography references (Snyder, J.P. 1987, USGS Professional Paper 1395) on polar azimuthal equidistant projections for which θ = −lon_E (with appropriate sign convention for westward-increasing azimuth) is definitional.

stronger_arguments.description: If the 0.9941 factor is a live empirical tune on distance calculations, that itself is an EXP-worthy discovery: it means the dome's distance engine is NOT running on θ = −lon_E — it is running on 0.9941 × (−lon_E). The 'unified' coordinate claim therefore doesn't apply to the production distance computation. Stronger than the current 'coexist without reconciliation' framing would be: 'In practice, distance_analysis.py never uses θ = −lon_E directly — it uses 0.9941 × Δθ. The V13 unified rule is not the rule the dome code actually runs on.' This is a more pointed attack on the 'unified' adjective in the claim.
sources: Dome's own distance_analysis.py (verified in detail_evidence: delta_t = np.radians(t2 - t1) * 0.9941)
Dome OPEN-003 (EW scale 0.9941 physical interpretation unresolved)

kernel_of_truth.description: The dome correctly identified and fixed a real bug in its own V9-era coordinate system. The solar-noon-UTC proxy for θ produced 180°+ errors in the Southern Hemisphere (Sydney being the canonical example, with the minimum angular distance being ≈176°). Recognizing that solar_noon_UTC ≈ 12 − lon_E/15h and therefore θ = −lon_E is mathematically correct and eliminated the catastrophic error. This is genuine model improvement — the dome author's V13 release is scientifically better than V12, and the V13→V12 transition is a clean example of self-correction on a discoverable bug.
kernel_of_truth.why_it_doesnt_save_claim: Fixing a self-inflicted bug in one's own coordinate system is maintenance, not prediction. The bug existed only because V9 introduced an unnecessary indirection (solar noon → θ); the direct quantity (geographic longitude) has been available since the 18th century. V13's 'discovery' amounts to recognizing the indirection was unnecessary. This is a valid engineering improvement but the identity θ = −lon_E is not derived from dome geometry — it is a coordinate convention that follows from the definition of longitude. Counting it as a 'confirmed prediction' requires promoting 'we fixed a bug we created' to the same status as 'we predicted a measurement before it was made', which is category inflation. The dome's own 2026-04-17 claim taxonomy (methodology.json 'retrospective_structural' vs 'prospective_confirmed') acknowledges this distinction at the API layer, though the visible wins.html badge still reads CONFIRMED 2026-03-28 without the category qualifier.

deeper_analysis: The 4 patches from the prior curmudgeon review (WIN-058-062-post-EXP-181) are all present and correct in the current WIN-058 body. The critical #win058b anchor now works (verified: id='win058b' at docs/index.html:1951). The 'status downgraded' claim is now appropriately softened to reflect what the change-file evidence actually shows. The 'three → at least three' hedge is in place, correctly accommodating ai_manifest.json registry_semantics as a potential 4th concession. The 'aggregate count' framing is now sharper — the integrity problem is explicitly the label collision (two different claims under one visible label), not a tally inflation (WIN-058's pending-badge sibling doesn't double-count against '69 confirmed'). The verdict 'Misleading' is correctly preserved — the underlying critique (θ = −lon_E is a coordinate convention, not a dome-derived prediction) is not affected by the registry-integrity acknowledgement the dome author shipped, and the patches did not reopen the verdict question. New holes found in this review are mostly copy-editing artifacts (the 'concession' singular/plural typo, the 183° vs 176° inconsistency, the hedge-then-firm count in adjacent sentences) or summary-to-detail mismatch (the finding's 'two incompatible angular conventions' overreaching what distance_analysis.py actually contains). None are verdict-changing. Recommended_action: minor_edit. If the decider wants to batch the six minor items into one patch, that's cleaner than six individual patches; alternatively, the two moderate items (176/183 inconsistency and finding-summary overreach) are the only ones that a hostile defender could weaponize and should be prioritized.

citations_verified: methodology.json registry_counts / timestamp_scope concessions — chg-20260417-1620-001 (classification: critical)
registry_integrity_notes block in docs/api/current/claim_index.json V51.1, last_updated 2026-04-17 — confirmed via change-file 002 (ai_manifest.json / review-response.html wave)
Sydney +32.5°/−151.2° — standard lat/lon (longitude of Sydney ≈ 151.2°E, consistent with the cited values)
citations_unchecked: 0.9941 EW scale factor live in distance_analysis.py — cited text quoted but the raw-text/ extracts in the clone do not include distance_analysis.py directly; relying on the prior curmudgeon's and analyst's attestation that this code string is present upstream. If this ever turns out to be paraphrased rather than verbatim, the whole 'coexist without reconciliation' argument weakens. Recommend a one-time spot-check next time the analyst pulls from the dome repo.

'69 confirmed' headline number — verified as of 2026-04-18 via daily_review.json (change file chg-20260418-1109-002); should be recomputed at build time if the dome author ever changes the visible registry count.
